get.description:
List WARP devices. Not supported when [multi-user mode](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-devices/warp/deployment/mdm-deployment/windows-multiuser/) is enabled for the account.

**Deprecated**: please use one of the following endpoints instead:
- GET /accounts/{account_id}/devices/physical-devices
- GET /accounts/{account_id}/devices/registrations

allow_mode_switch.description: Whether to allow the user to switch WARP between modes.

allow_updates.description: Whether to receive update notifications when a new version of the client is available.

allowed_to_leave.description: Whether to allow devices to leave the organization.

auto_connect.description: The amount of time in seconds to reconnect after having been disabled.

captive_portal.description: Turn on the captive portal after the specified amount of time.

disable_auto_fallback.description: If the `dns_server` field of a fallback domain is not present, the client will fall back to a best guess of the default/system DNS resolvers unless this policy option is set to `true`.

exclude_office_ips.description: Whether to add Microsoft IPs to Split Tunnel exclusions.

lan_allow_minutes.description: The amount of time in minutes a user is allowed access to their LAN. A value of 0 will allow LAN access until the next WARP reconnection, such as a reboot or a laptop waking from sleep. Note that this field is omitted from the response if null or unset.

lan_allow_subnet_size.description: The size of the subnet for the local access network. Note that this field is omitted from the response if null or unset.

register_interface_ip_with_dns.description: Determines if the operating system will register WARP's local interface IP with your on-premises DNS server.

sccm_vpn_boundary_support.description: Determines whether the WARP client indicates to SCCM that it is inside a VPN boundary. (Windows only).

support_url.description: The URL to launch when the Send Feedback button is clicked.

switch_locked.description: Whether to allow the user to turn off the WARP switch and disconnect the client.

tunnel_protocol.description: Determines which tunnel protocol to use.

200.description: Update the default device settings profile response.

4XX.description: Update the default device settings profile response failure.

patch.summary: Update the default device settings profile

post.description: Creates a device settings profile to be applied to certain devices matching the criteria.

allow_mode_switch.description: Whether to allow the user to switch WARP between modes.

allow_updates.description: Whether to receive update notifications when a new version of the client is available.

allowed_to_leave.description: Whether to allow devices to leave the organization.

auto_connect.description: The amount of time in seconds to reconnect after having been disabled.

captive_portal.description: Turn on the captive portal after the specified amount of time.

disable_auto_fallback.description: If the `dns_server` field of a fallback domain is not present, the client will fall back to a best guess of the default/system DNS resolvers unless this policy option is set to `true`.

enabled.description: Whether the policy will be applied to matching devices.

exclude_office_ips.description: Whether to add Microsoft IPs to Split Tunnel exclusions.

lan_allow_minutes.description: The amount of time in minutes a user is allowed access to their LAN. A value of 0 will allow LAN access until the next WARP reconnection, such as a reboot or a laptop waking from sleep. Note that this field is omitted from the response if null or unset.

lan_allow_subnet_size.description: The size of the subnet for the local access network. Note that this field is omitted from the response if null or unset.

match.description: The wirefilter expression to match devices. Available values: "identity.email", "identity.groups.id", "identity.groups.name", "identity.groups.email", "identity.service_token_uuid", "identity.saml_attributes", "network", "os.name", "os.version".

name.description: The name of the device settings profile.

precedence.description: The precedence of the policy. Lower values indicate higher precedence. Policies will be evaluated in ascending order of this field.

register_interface_ip_with_dns.description: Determines if the operating system will register WARP's local interface IP with your on-premises DNS server.

sccm_vpn_boundary_support.description: Determines whether the WARP client indicates to SCCM that it is inside a VPN boundary. (Windows only).

support_url.description: The URL to launch when the Send Feedback button is clicked.

switch_locked.description: Whether to allow the user to turn off the WARP switch and disconnect the client.

tunnel_protocol.description: Determines which tunnel protocol to use.

post.description: Creates a new device posture rule.

description.description: The description of the device posture rule.
description.example: The rule for admin serial numbers

expiration.description: Sets the expiration time for a posture check result. If empty, the result remains valid until it is overwritten by new data from the WARP client.

name.description: The name of the device posture rule.

schedule.description: Polling frequency for the WARP client posture check. Default: `5m` (poll every five minutes). Minimum: `1m`.

type.description: The type of device posture rule.

**Deprecated:** please use GET /accounts/{account_id}/devices/registrations/{registration_id}/override_codes instead.

commands.description: List of device-level commands to execute

interfaces.description: List of interfaces to capture packets on

max-file-size-mb.description: Maximum file size (in MB) for the capture file. Specifies the maximum file size of the warp-diag zip artifact that can be uploaded. If the zip artifact exceeds the specified max file size, it will NOT be uploaded

packet-size-bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes to save for each packet

test-all-routes.description: Test an IP address from all included or excluded ranges. Tests an IP address from all included or excluded ranges. Essentially the same as running 'route get <ip>'' and collecting the results. This option may increase the time taken to collect the warp-diag

command_type.description: Type of command to execute on the device

device_id.description: Unique identifier for the physical device

registration_id.description: Unique identifier for the device registration. Required for multi-user devices to target the correct user session.

os_version_extra.description: Additional operating system version details. For Windows, the UBR (Update Build Revision). For Mac or iOS, the Product Version Extra. For Linux, the distribution name and version.

requireAll.description: Whether to check all disks for encryption.

cn.description: Certificate Common Name. This may include one or more variables in the ${ } notation. Only ${serial_number} and ${hostname} are valid variables.

check_private_key.description: Confirm the certificate was not imported from another device. We recommend keeping this enabled unless the certificate was deployed without a private key.

extended_key_usage.description: List of values indicating purposes for which the certificate public key can be used.

subject_alternative_names.description: List of certificate Subject Alternative Names.

update_window_days.description: Number of days that the antivirus should be updated within.

last_seen.description: For more details on last seen, please refer to the Crowdstrike documentation.

state.description: For more details on state, please refer to the Crowdstrike documentation.

eid_last_seen.description: For more details on eid last seen, refer to the Tanium documentation.

risk_level.description: For more details on risk level, refer to the Tanium documentation.

total_score.description: For more details on total score, refer to the Tanium documentation.

score.description: A value between 0-100 assigned to devices set by the 3rd party posture provider.

teams-devices_input.description: The value to be checked against.

avgHttpAvailabilityPct.description: percentage availability for all HTTP test results in response

avgTracerouteAvailabilityPct.description: percentage availability for all traceroutes results in response

description.description: the test description defined during configuration

enabled.description: if true, then the test will run on targeted devices. Else, the test will not run.

interval.description: The interval at which the synthetic application test is set to run.

method.description: for HTTP, the method to use when running the test

name.description: name given to this test

default.description: Whether the policy is the default for the account

count.description: Number of records in the response.

page.description: The page size number of the response.

per_page.description: The limit for the number of records in the response.

interval.description: The interval at which the Traceroute synthetic application test is set to run.

items.description: Registration ID. Equal to Device ID except for accounts which enabled [multi-user mode](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-devices/warp/deployment/mdm-deployment/windows-multiuser/).

additionalProperties.description: Command argument value as a string

device_id.description: Identifier for the device associated with the command

registration_id.description: Unique identifier for the device registration

active_registrations.description: The number of active registrations for the device. Active registrations are those which haven't been revoked or deleted.

created_at.description: The RFC3339 timestamp when the device was created.

deleted_at.description: The RFC3339 timestamp when the device was deleted.

hardware_id.description: A string that uniquely identifies the hardware or virtual machine (VM).

id.description: The unique ID of the device.

last_seen_at.description: The RFC3339 timestamp when the device was last seen.

last_seen_registration.description: The last seen registration for the device.

last_seen_user.description: The last user to use the WARP device.

model.description: The model name of the device.

name.description: The name of the device.

os_version.description: The device operating system version number.

os_version_extra.description: Additional operating system version details. For Windows, the UBR (Update Build Revision). For Mac or iOS, the Product Version Extra. For Linux, the distribution name and version.

public_ip.description: **Deprecated**: IP information is provided by DEX - see https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/resources/zero_trust/subresources/dex/subresources/fleet_status/subresources/devices/methods/list/

updated_at.description: The RFC3339 timestamp when the device was last updated.

mode.description: The mode under which the WARP client is run

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

items.description: Registration ID. Equal to Device ID except for accounts which enabled [multi-user mode](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-devices/warp/deployment/mdm-deployment/windows-multiuser/).

address.description: The address in CIDR format to include in the tunnel. If `address` is present, `host` must not be present.

description.description: A description of the Split Tunnel item, displayed in the client UI.
description.example: Include testing domains in the tunnel

host.description: The domain name to include in the tunnel. If `host` is present, `address` must not be present.

description.description: An optional description of the Device IP profile.

enabled.description: Whether the Device IP profile is enabled.

match.description: The wirefilter expression to match registrations. Available values: "identity.name", "identity.email", "identity.groups.id", "identity.groups.name", "identity.groups.email", "identity.saml_attributes".

name.description: A user-friendly name for the Device IP profile.

precedence.description: The precedence of the Device IP profile. Lower values indicate higher precedence. Device IP profile will be evaluated in ascending order of this field.

quota.description: The remaining number of commands that can be initiated for an account

quota_usage.description: The number of commands that have been initiated for an account

reset_time.description: The time when the quota resets

count.description: Number of records in the response.

cursor.description: Opaque token to request the next set of records.

per_page.description: The limit for the number of records in the response.

registration_id.description: Unique identifier for the device registration

interval.description: The interval between each posture check with the third-party API. Use `m` for minutes (e.g. `5m`) and `h` for hours (e.g. `12h`).

name.description: The name of the device posture integration.

type.description: The type of device posture integration.

hostname.description: The hostname of the machine the event is from

serial_number.description: The serial number of the machine the event is from

teams-devices_exclude_request.description: List of routes excluded in the WARP client's tunnel. Both 'exclude' and 'include' cannot be set in the same request.

teams-devices_v4_response_message.description: A message which can be returned in either the 'errors' or 'messages' fields in a v4 API response.

mode.description: The mode to run the WARP client under.

port.description: The port number when used with proxy mode.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

allow_mode_switch.description: Whether to allow the user to switch WARP between modes.

allow_updates.description: Whether to receive update notifications when a new version of the client is available.

allowed_to_leave.description: Whether to allow devices to leave the organization.

auto_connect.description: The amount of time in seconds to reconnect after having been disabled.

captive_portal.description: Turn on the captive portal after the specified amount of time.

default.description: Whether the policy is the default policy for an account.

disable_auto_fallback.description: If the `dns_server` field of a fallback domain is not present, the client will fall back to a best guess of the default/system DNS resolvers unless this policy option is set to `true`.

enabled.description: Whether the policy will be applied to matching devices.

exclude_office_ips.description: Whether to add Microsoft IPs to Split Tunnel exclusions.

lan_allow_minutes.description: The amount of time in minutes a user is allowed access to their LAN. A value of 0 will allow LAN access until the next WARP reconnection, such as a reboot or a laptop waking from sleep. Note that this field is omitted from the response if null or unset.

lan_allow_subnet_size.description: The size of the subnet for the local access network. Note that this field is omitted from the response if null or unset.

match.description: The wirefilter expression to match devices. Available values: "identity.email", "identity.groups.id", "identity.groups.name", "identity.groups.email", "identity.service_token_uuid", "identity.saml_attributes", "network", "os.name", "os.version".

name.description: The name of the device settings profile.

precedence.description: The precedence of the policy. Lower values indicate higher precedence. Policies will be evaluated in ascending order of this field.

register_interface_ip_with_dns.description: Determines if the operating system will register WARP's local interface IP with your on-premises DNS server.

sccm_vpn_boundary_support.description: Determines whether the WARP client indicates to SCCM that it is inside a VPN boundary. (Windows only).

support_url.description: The URL to launch when the Send Feedback button is clicked.

switch_locked.description: Whether to allow the user to turn off the WARP switch and disconnect the client.

tunnel_protocol.description: Determines which tunnel protocol to use.

eligible.description: Whether the device is eligible for remote captures

ineligibleReason.description: If the device is not eligible, the reason why.

registrationId.description: Device registration identifier (UUID v4). On multi-user devices, this uniquely identifies a user's registration on the device.

deviceName.description: name of the device associated with this network path response

hops.description: an array of the hops taken by the device to reach the end destination

disable_for_time.description: Sets the time limit, in seconds, that a user can use an override code to bypass WARP.

external_emergency_signal_enabled.description: Controls whether the external emergency disconnect feature is enabled.

external_emergency_signal_fingerprint.description: The SHA256 fingerprint (64 hexadecimal characters) of the HTTPS server certificate for the external_emergency_signal_url. If provided, the WARP client will use this value to verify the server's identity. The device will ignore any response if the server's certificate fingerprint does not exactly match this value.

external_emergency_signal_interval.description: The interval at which the WARP client fetches the emergency disconnect signal, formatted as a duration string (e.g., "5m", "2m30s", "1h"). Minimum 30 seconds.

external_emergency_signal_url.description: The HTTPS URL from which to fetch the emergency disconnect signal. Must use HTTPS and have an IPv4 or IPv6 address as the host.

gateway_proxy_enabled.description: Enable gateway proxy filtering on TCP.

gateway_udp_proxy_enabled.description: Enable gateway proxy filtering on UDP.

root_certificate_installation_enabled.description: Enable installation of cloudflare managed root certificate.

p50.description: p50 observed in the time period

p90.description: p90 observed in the time period

p95.description: p95 observed in the time period

p99.description: p99 observed in the time period

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

sha256.description: The SHA-256 hash of the TLS certificate presented by the host found at tls_sockaddr. If absent, regular certificate verification (trusted roots, valid timestamp, etc) will be used to validate the certificate.

tls_sockaddr.description: A network address of the form "host:port" that the WARP client will use to detect the presence of a TLS host.

teams-devices_devices_get_device_param_i.description: Comma-separated list of additional information that should be included in the device response. Supported values are: "last_seen_registration.policy".
teams-devices_devices_get_device_param_i.name: include

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Filter by the last_seen timestamp - returns only devices last seen after this timestamp.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: seen_after

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: The maximum number of devices to return in a single response.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: per_page

teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.description: Filter by the last_seen timestamp - returns only registrations last seen before this timestamp.
teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.name: seen_before

teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.description: Filter by the last_seen timestamp - returns only registrations last seen after this timestamp.
teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.name: seen_after

teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.description: Comma-separated list of additional information that should be included in the registration response. Supported values are: "policy".
teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.name: include

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Comma-separated list of additional information that should be included in the device response. Supported values are: "last_seen_registration.policy".
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: include

teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.description: Opaque token indicating the starting position when requesting the next set of records. A cursor value can be obtained from the result_info.cursor field in the response.
teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.name: cursor

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Include or exclude devices with active registrations. The default is "only" - return only devices with active registrations.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: active_registrations

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Filter by the last seen user's email.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: last_seen_user.email

teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.description: The maximum number of devices to return in a single response.
teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.name: per_page

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Filter by the last_seen timestamp - returns only devices last seen before this timestamp.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: seen_before

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Opaque token indicating the starting position when requesting the next set of records. A cursor value can be obtained from the result_info.cursor field in the response.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: cursor

teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.description: The registration field to order results by.
teams-devices_devices_list_registrations.name: sort_by

teams-devices_devices_get_registration_p.description: Comma-separated list of additional information that should be included in the registration response. Supported values are: "policy".
teams-devices_devices_get_registration_p.name: include

teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.description: Filter by a one or more device IDs.
teams-devices_devices_list_devices_param.name: id
